title: eriostemon australasius subsp. banksii
intro: Eriostemon australasius subsp. banksii, also known as Banks' waxflower, is a plant species native to Australia. It belongs to the Rutaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, with benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. Eriostemon australasius subsp. banksii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
